CSV data analysis - unique values in 'Year' column

Data contains mostly anonymized codes (A, B, C...Z, AA, BB, etc.) with rare real-world like entries (euromessage/push) and apparent typos (l.K, l.M, lI); characteristic of synthetic/test data rather than production Google Analytics export

pi_items.source: CSV column verification and sample data inspection

privileged_info_summary: The website_analytics.csv dataset contains 250 rows of synthetic web traffic data (not 249 as claimed) with 13 columns: Source / Medium, Year, Month of the year, Users, New Users, Sessions, Bounce Rate, Pageviews, Avg. Session Duration, Conversion Rate (%), Transactions, Revenue, and Quantity Sold. The data covers years 2019 and 2020. All columns required for the task (Source / Medium, Pageviews, Bounce Rate for the table visual, and Year for the slicer) are present and valid. The data is synthetic, indicated by mostly single/double-letter source codes. The task runs on Windows 11 with Power BI Desktop installed.
